text:
As 2020 approached its conclusion, businesses continued to face persistent and evolving challenges. Across the UK, non-essential organisations operating in Tier 4 regions were once again forced to suspend operations, placing additional pressure on companies that had already endured a difficult year. The prolonged impact of COVID-19 showed no signs of easing, with disruptions expected to continue into the new year.

This period also prompted organisations to reassess critical internal priorities. Issues such as cybersecurity resilience, employee mental health, and workplace equality moved to the forefront of business agendas. The Black Lives Matter movement further emphasized the importance of diversity and inclusion, encouraging companies to examine and address unconscious bias within their structures.

Broader structural changes reshaped how businesses operate. Remote working rapidly became standard practice, while many organisations reassessed core operational models. Simultaneously, the UK's exit from the European Union introduced new strategic considerations. Collectively, these factors made 2020 one of the most demanding years for modern business.

To better understand the impact of these challenges, particularly relating to COVID-19 and workplace training, a survey was conducted among professionals from a wide range of industries. The findings reveal key insights into current pressures and anticipated challenges for 2021.

Impact of COVID-19

60.8% reported a negative impact on their business

45% experienced slight to significant declines, compared with 22.8% who saw growth

Top challenges in 2020

Staff absences due to illness or self-isolation

Managing re-opening during and after COVID-19

Staff training

Top challenges moving into 2021

Employee mental health and wellbeing

Health and safety management

Financial constraints, including reduced budgets

Employee Health & Safety

62.8% use workplace meetings to stay informed on industry updates

Mental health support and fostering a strong safety culture are primary concerns

91.8% are aware of legal obligations related to health, safety, HR, and compliance

Training importance was rated 9.3 out of 10

Workplace Training

67.4% deliver training through eLearning

55.4% renew training on a needs-based schedule rather than fixed intervals

Training quality is the most important selection criterion

Key challenges include cost, consistency, and employee completion rates

metadata.notes: English workplace report about COVID-19 impact and workplace training - AI generated
